title: What I actually need in relationships
subtitle: A journal prompt for naming the needs you carry without asking for
excerpt: A journal prompt for identifying what you actually need in relationships versus what you settle for or perform. Most people have never named their needs clearly enough to ask for them.

practice.whenToUse: When you feel consistently unmet in a relationship but can't articulate what you're missing, or when resentment is building without a clear target

content.text: Most people can name what bothers them in relationships faster than they can name what they actually need. This prompt works in the other direction. The aim is to get specific enough that you could actually ask for it.
Think of a time in a relationship when you felt fully met. What was the other person doing? What were they not doing?
Think of a time when you felt consistently unseen. What was missing? Not who was wrong. What was missing.
What do you need most in a close relationship? Choose one word, then three. Then write a sentence that a real person could act on.
Which of your needs do you suppress or perform around? Which ones do you pretend you don't have?
Have you asked for what you need in this way before? If you haven't, what stops you?
